substi_correct.solution:
Inductive substi (s : tm) (x : string) : tm -> tm -> Prop :=
  | s_var1 :
      substi s x (tm_var x) s
  | s_var2 : forall y,
      x <> y ->
      substi s x (tm_var y) (tm_var y)
  | s_abs1 : forall t1 T,
      substi s x <{\x:T, t1}> <{\x:T, t1}>
  | s_abs2 : forall y t1 t1' T,
      x <> y ->
      substi s x t1 t1' ->
      substi s x <{\y:T, t1}> <{\y:T, t1'}>
  | s_app : forall t1 t1' t2 t2',
      substi s x t1 t1' ->
      substi s x t2 t2' ->
      substi s x <{t1 t2}> <{t1' t2'}>
  | s_true :
      substi s x <{true}> <{true}>
  | s_false :
      substi s x <{false}> <{false}>
  | s_if : forall t1 t1' t2 t2' t3 t3',
      substi s x t1 t1' ->
      substi s x t2 t2' ->
      substi s x t3 t3' ->
      substi s x <{if t1 then t2 else t3}> <{if t1' then t2' else t3'}>.

Theorem substi_correct : forall s x t t',
  <{ [x:=s]t }> = t' <-> substi s x t t'.
Proof.
  intros s x t. split.
  - generalize dependent t'. induction t; intros t' H; simpl in H.
    + (* var *) destruct (String.eqb_spec x s0); subst; auto.
    + (* app *) subst. constructor; auto.
    + (* abs *) destruct (String.eqb_spec x s0); subst; auto.
      constructor; auto.
    + (* true *) subst. constructor.
    + (* false *) subst. constructor.
    + (* if *) subst. constructor; auto.
  - intros H. induction H; simpl;
    try reflexivity;
    try (rewrite String.eqb_refl; reflexivity);
    try (apply String.eqb_neq in H; rewrite H; reflexivity);
    try (rewrite IHsubsti; reflexivity);
    try (rewrite IHsubsti1; rewrite IHsubsti2; reflexivity);
    try (rewrite IHsubsti1; rewrite IHsubsti2; rewrite IHsubsti3; reflexivity);
    try (apply String.eqb_neq in H; rewrite H; rewrite IHsubsti; reflexivity).
Qed.
substi_correct.explanation: Define substitution as an inductive relation with constructors for each term form, then prove equivalence with the functional definition.

step_example5.solution:
Lemma step_example5 :
       <{idBBBB idBB idB}>
  -->* idB.
Proof.
  eapply multi_step.
  { apply ST_App1. apply ST_AppAbs. auto. }
  simpl.
  eapply multi_step.
  { apply ST_AppAbs. auto. }
  simpl.
  apply multi_refl.
Qed.

Lemma step_example5_with_normalize :
       <{idBBBB idBB idB}>
  -->* idB.
Proof. normalize. Qed.
step_example5.explanation: idBBBB idBB idB reduces: first apply idBBBB to idBB (yielding idBB), then apply idBB to idB (yielding idB).

typing_example_2_full.solution:
Example typing_example_2_full :
 <{ empty |--
    \x:Bool,
       \y:Bool->Bool,
          (y (y x)) \in
    Bool -> (Bool -> Bool) -> Bool }>.
Proof.
  apply T_Abs. apply T_Abs.
  apply T_App with (T2 := <{ Bool }>).
  - apply T_Var. rewrite update_neq; [| discriminate]. apply update_eq.
  - apply T_App with (T2 := <{ Bool }>).
    + apply T_Var. rewrite update_neq; [| discriminate]. apply update_eq.
    + apply T_Var. rewrite update_neq; [| discriminate].
      rewrite update_neq; [| discriminate]. apply update_eq.
Qed.
typing_example_2_full.explanation: Manually construct the typing derivation using T_Abs, T_App, and T_Var with explicit context lookups.

typing_example_3.solution:
Example typing_example_3 :
  exists T,
   <{ empty |--
      \x:Bool->Bool,
         \y:Bool->Bool,
            \z:Bool,
               (y (x z)) \in
      T }>.
Proof.
  exists <{ (Bool -> Bool) -> (Bool -> Bool) -> Bool -> Bool }>.
  eauto 20.
Qed.
typing_example_3.explanation: The type is (Bool->Bool) -> (Bool->Bool) -> Bool -> Bool. eauto can find the derivation automatically.

typing_nonexample_3.solution:
Example typing_nonexample_3 :
  ~ (exists S T,
      <{ empty |--
          \x:S, x x \in T }>).
Proof.
  intros [S [T Hc]].
  inversion Hc; subst; clear Hc.
  inversion H4; subst; clear H4.
  inversion H2; subst; clear H2.
  inversion H5; subst; clear H5.
  rewrite H1 in H3. injection H3 as H3.
  (* S = S -> T2, which is impossible for finite types *)
  induction S; discriminate.
Qed.
typing_nonexample_3.explanation: Self-application \x:S, x x requires x to have type S and also S -> T, making S = S -> T, which is impossible for finite types.
